taskDescription: Arrange the segments of the GIT from proximal to distal based on their histological distinguishing features
paragraphs: Oesophagus — stratified squamous epithelium, submucosal glands, muscularis transitions from skeletal to smooth muscle
Stomach (fundus) — simple columnar epithelium, parietal cells secreting HCl, chief cells secreting pepsinogen
Stomach (pylorus) — G-cells producing gastrin, prominent mucous glands
Duodenum — Brunner's glands in submucosa secreting alkaline mucus, broad leaf-like villi
Jejunum — tallest plicae circulares, tall finger-like villi, moderate goblet cells
Ileum — Peyer's patches (aggregated lymphoid follicles), short finger-like villi, many goblet cells
Large intestine — no villi, abundant goblet cells, crypts only, teniae coli in muscularis externa
